mappings.control_id: Providers of high-risk AI must implement a risk management system covering all reasonably foreseeable risks
mappings.notes: Prompt injection must be identified, analysed, and mitigated in the risk management system

mappings.control_id: High-risk AI systems must be resilient to adversarial inputs and attempts to alter outputs or performance
mappings.notes: Robustness against prompt injection is a technical compliance requirement — not optional

mappings.control_id: Providers of systemic risk GPAI models must conduct adversarial testing to identify and mitigate systemic risks
mappings.notes: Prompt injection adversarial testing is a binding obligation for systemic risk models

AI systems monitored in operation — runtime injection detection as AIMS monitoring control

mappings.control_name: Verify all user input validated against an allowlist or rejected

mappings.control_name: Verify that HTTP request parts are validated, sanitised, or rejected

mappings.control_name: Verify output encoding prevents injection attacks
mappings.notes: LLM output encoding before passing to downstream renderers or interpreters

mappings.control_name: Verify application protects against OS command injection
mappings.notes: LLM-generated content validated before execution in any shell or interpreter context

mappings.control_name: Threat modelling of all data flows
mappings.notes: LLM data flows threat-modelled — all injection paths identified and documented

mappings.control_name: Verify business logic limits prevent abuse of LLM functions
mappings.notes: Business logic controls preventing prompt injection from triggering unauthorised actions
All inputs to LLMs connected to OT systems validated for integrity — crafted inputs rejected
LLMs granted minimum necessary access to OT data — cannot read or write to control systems without explicit scoping
Enforcement of permitted use of LLM within OT context — out-of-scope requests rejected at the gateway layer
LLM interactions logged with user identity — injection attempts attributable to specific sessions
Ability to terminate LLM sessions immediately on detection of suspicious behaviour

mappings.control_id: Malicious code and logic attacks via IT/OT convergence

mappings.control_id: Identify threats, vulnerabilities, and impacts for all OT systems

mappings.control_id: Defense-in-depth network architecture with validated data flows

mappings.control_id: Injection blast radius proportional to credential scope
mappings.notes: Apply least-privilege to all LLM application credentials

mappings.control_id: Injected session can use stolen long-lived tokens for extended period
mappings.notes: Rotate all LLM application tokens; implement short-lived token pattern

mappings.control_name: Design software to meet security requirements — adversarial input
mappings.notes: Threat model the AI pipeline for adversarial input vectors including direct and indirect prompt injection; document mitigating design decisions

mappings.control_name: Review the software for security vulnerabilities — model behaviour
mappings.notes: Review model outputs and system behaviour for unexpected responses to adversarial inputs; include injection bypass scenarios in code and design reviews

mappings.control_name: Test for security vulnerabilities — adversarial / red-team
mappings.notes: Conduct adversarial testing (red-teaming) against prompt injection vectors before each production release; cover direct, indirect, and multimodal injection paths
Establish procedures to identify prompt injection incidents in production including monitoring, triage, and confirmation workflows

mappings.control_name: Malicious Code Protection — adversarial AI inputs
mappings.notes: Extend malicious code protection to detect and block adversarial inputs including direct and indirect prompt injection payloads
Validate all inputs to LLM inference endpoints; enforce structural separation between instruction and data contexts; reject known injection patterns
Include prompt injection vectors in vulnerability scanning programme; conduct regular automated and manual injection testing
Include prompt injection scenarios in penetration testing engagements; cover direct, indirect, and multimodal injection paths

mappings.control_name: Protection and Prevention — adversarial input controls
mappings.notes: Implement ICT security controls to detect and block adversarial inputs including direct and indirect prompt injection; treat as a mandatory protection measure

mappings.control_name: Digital Operational Resilience Testing — AI red-teaming
mappings.notes: Include prompt injection scenarios in threat-led penetration testing (TLPT); cover direct, indirect, and multimodal injection vectors targeting financial AI services
Deploy detection mechanisms for prompt injection attempts; monitor inference requests for adversarial patterns and alert security operations
Share prompt injection threat intelligence with sector peers through DORA information sharing arrangements
